trust_model.statement: The kernel, filesystem implementation, base interpreter and standard library, invoking account, and every process able to write the repository, isolated environment, or results roots are trusted. A WM-001 run has exclusive use of those paths; no cooperating or malicious same-principal writer may run concurrently. Every conforming sealed-runtime invocation acquires one repository-global nonblocking advisory lock for its complete child lifetime; QA-only preformal commands remain covered by the exclusive-use assumption. The lock coordinates trusted harness processes but is not a security boundary against noncooperating same-principal writers. Hashes, descriptor checks, pre/post inventories, and no-replace publication detect accidental or persistent drift and application-level overwrite; they do not provide tamper resistance against the account or environment owner, transient mutate-and-restore attacks, privileged actors, or a compromised kernel. Immutable means protocol-level append-only/no-replace evidence under this trust boundary, not fs-verity, read-only media, a TPM, an external signer, or external attestation.

experiment.title: Causal world-model learning, behavioral improvement, retention, and restart

revision.reason: The frozen v1.17.0 harness passed every prospective gate and completed its sole full-budget development producer, independent audit, closure, fresh-runtime reopen, ten-command preformal report, formal-input preflight, accepted binding, and final stop/go sequence. Its binding-keyed result-free rehearsal child returned zero and the standard-library launcher published an authentic accepted, outer-finalized terminal with empty stderr and no formal path. The mandatory separate verifier then rejected that same valid stdout because it read matrix_contract_sha256 from development_qualification even though formal-binding v10 deliberately omits and explicitly forbids that field. Unit and subprocess fixtures had invented the forbidden member and generated their stdout from it while bypassing strict binding verification. Version 1.18.0 makes the sealed protocol identity already checked by verify_binding the independent verifier's matrix authority, removes the nonexistent lookup and every invented fixture field, asserts the exact production v10 development projection, rejects a wrong emitted digest, and requires bytes accepted by the production launcher to pass the independent verifier. Formal-binding v10, raw-result v9, protocol v9, preformal-report v2, and formal-launch v3 remain unchanged. Version 1.18.0 uses fresh canonical paths, environments, runtime custody, derivation domain, and master seeds. The model, learning algorithm, optimizer, planner, controller, budgets, controls, metrics, thresholds, exclusions, killing gates, scientific blocks, kernel sources, matrix contract, and raw-result contract are unchanged.
revision.outcome_use: The v1.17.0 development producer, qualification, audit, closure, preformal report, accepted binding, formal-input preflight, authenticated accepted rehearsal, and outer completions remain immutable terminal engineering evidence. Development evidence is permanently claim-ineligible, and no v1.17.0 formal root, launch, producer, outcome, audit, semantic review, or adjudication exists or may be created. Precommitted automation processed the development result, but no v1.17.0 K3-K6 value or other scientific outcome or performance value was manually opened, printed, summarized, compared, thresholded, selected on, or used to choose any v1.18.0 scientific or harness setting. Repair selection used only source and schema bytes, authenticated result-free identities and sizes, canonical receipt and terminal metadata, exact formal-path absence, and the read-only counterfactual showing that substituting the protocol-verified matrix identity for the nonexistent lookup makes the complete independent package verification pass. No v1.17.0 evidence may authorize, block, rerun, resume, upgrade, repair, bind, or complete v1.18.0. Version 1.18.0 master seeds were selected before any v1.18.0 development or formal outcome by the first four bytes of SHA-256('WM-001|1.18.0|' + lane + '-master|' + decimal(index)). The complete v1.18.0 master and derived-stream universe has no collision with any exposed v1.0.0, v1.2.0, v1.3.0, v1.4.0, v1.5.0, v1.6.0, v1.7.0, v1.8.0, v1.9.0, v1.10.0, v1.11.0, v1.12.0, v1.13.0, v1.14.0, v1.15.0, v1.16.0, or v1.17.0 master or derived stream, and both verifier implementations use the exact same historical master tuples.

experiment.supersession_rule: Any semantic, seed, budget, metric, threshold, control, or gate change creates a new protocol version and invalidates all formal outcomes collected under an earlier or unbound version.

claim: On the sealed WM-001 Pendulum/oscillator fixture, the same Prospect A/B agent collects identified task-A real experience, changes one shared persistent probabilistic world model because of exactly that experience, improves held-out task-A prediction beyond frozen, target-permuted, and separately verified learned-oscillator controls, improves executed task-A return beyond cold, frozen, and that learned-oscillator control, learns the conflicting observed-context task B without task-specific model copies, retains the task-A gain through replay under demonstrated naive interference, and reproduces the retained state and one predeclared 200-step continuation per task after a same-machine, same-dependency fresh-process restore.

null_hypothesis.statement: Task-A experience-linked world-model learning does not cause reliable held-out predictive improvement beyond matched frozen, corrupted-target, and validated oscillator-trained controls; executed-control improvement beyond cold, frozen, oscillator-trained, and random controls; second-task plasticity; first-task retention; and fresh-process persistence.
null_hypothesis.rejection_rule: Reject only if every killing gate K0 through K7 passes in order in the formal lane. Passing a later numerical check cannot rescue an earlier failed gate.

establishes_if_passed: one continuous causal lifecycle on two contextual variants of Pendulum-v1
persistent learned world-model state used by later planning
plasticity and replay-based retention under deliberately demonstrated interference
component-complete checkpoint and same-machine, same-dependency fresh-process parity
does_not_establish: general intelligence or broad task generalization
novelty over PETS, CEM, replay, or other established model-based control methods
pixel, language, audio, or multimodal representation learning
superiority to published state of the art

causal_chain: initialize one cold shared model, record its parameter digest, and fork an isolated cold oscillator control
collect task-A real experience through the authoritative agent lifecycle
collect real experience from the independent phase oscillator in isolated custody
prepare candidate weights from the exact task-A transition IDs without mutating the live model
validate ancestry and candidate state, then atomically commit the candidate
train the isolated oscillator control with the task-A optimizer schedule and verify its own-process held-out predictive gain
evaluate frozen held-out task-A prediction and executed MPC behavior
collect task-B real experience through the same lifecycle and shared model
train task B with balanced task-A replay through the same weights and heads
demonstrate that a matched B-only learner measurably forgets A
evaluate B plasticity and A retention with learning disabled
checkpoint every declared stateful component and restore in a fresh process
persist both live and restored evaluation traces and reproduce identities, predictions, actions, and executed returns by independently reopening them
evaluation_scheduling_rule: A paired evaluation condition may execute after a later causal phase only when it reloads immutable content-addressed bytes for the declared checkpoint, resets the exact paired environment and planner RNG seeds, and has learning and replay writes disabled. Scheduling does not change the condition's causal checkpoint identity.

environment.termination: No natural terminal state; the wrapper emits truncation at exactly 200 accepted environment actions.
environment.reward: The unmodified Pendulum-v1 reward computed from physical state and applied torque. The primary behavior metric is the undiscounted sum of 200 rewards.
environment.source_guard: The formal binding must record the exact Gymnasium version, installed distribution hash, wrapper source hash, and a conformance-test digest for all semantic parameters above.

irrelevant_control_environment.purpose: A valid and learnable real-evidence process that is causally independent of Pendulum task A while sharing the three-value observation interface.
irrelevant_control_environment.reset: SHA-256(source_id + ':' + decimal reset seed) deterministically maps its first and second unsigned 64-bit words to phase in [-pi, pi) and angular frequency in [0.5, 1.5).
irrelevant_control_environment.dynamics: theta_next = IEEE remainder(theta + 0.05 * angular_frequency, 2*pi); angular_frequency is constant.
irrelevant_control_environment.action: The seeded intended action remains recorded but is ignored by the process; canonical applied action is exactly 0.0.
irrelevant_control_environment.termination: No natural terminal state; truncation occurs at exactly 200 accepted actions.
irrelevant_control_environment.isolation: Its custody, real transition IDs, receipts, optimizer state, model checkpoint, and RNG streams are isolated from the A/B agent. They may be used only by the irrelevant control and must be absent from the retained K7 checkpoint.

tasks.shared_parameter_constraint: Both tasks must use the identical ensemble members, trunks, output heads, optimizer slots, and model version stream. The context scalar may be an input; task-keyed models, adapters, heads, routing tables, or checkpoint copies are forbidden.
tasks.irrelevant_branch_constraint: The irrelevant arm uses an exact cold compound-state fork of the same architecture and optimizer. It is a causal control, not part of the persistent A/B agent: its experience, optimizer, receipt, and after-irrelevant checkpoint must not enter the A/B replay stream or final K7 checkpoint.
tasks.interference_requirement: The matched naive B-only update must cause a statistically reliable task-A return loss. If it does not, WM-001 has not tested retention and stops at K5.

encoder.input: physical_observation concatenated with observed task context

fixed_scaling.rule: All divisors are constants in this protocol. No evaluation statistic may fit or update a normalizer.

world_model.rollout_projection: After adding predicted deltas, project the predicted (cos(theta), sin(theta)) pair onto the unit circle using max(L2_norm, 1e-8); clip predicted angular velocity to [-8, 8]; carry context unchanged.

uncertainty.planner_use: Reported in predictive evaluation but not used as a reward, penalty, acquisition signal, or rollout sample in WM-001. The planner consumes deterministic ensemble-mean next-state and reward predictions.

forbidden_shortcuts: calling the real environment transition from learned-model MPC
reading hidden theta or environment internals in the learned condition
training a task-specific model or head
using outcome-time data in action-time predictions

optimizer.name: AdamW
optimizer.bootstrap: Each ensemble member samples training transition IDs with replacement from the phase-eligible replay population using its declared member seed.
optimizer.loss: Mean normalized-target Gaussian negative log likelihood over members and output dimensions.

planner.model_adapter: At every imagined step, return the arithmetic mean of the five members' predicted physical-state deltas and reward, apply the declared rollout projection, and carry context unchanged.
planner.initial_action_distribution: The exact CEMPlanner initialization: independent Gaussian actions with zero mean and unit standard deviation, projected through the bounded action spec to [-2, 2].
planner.elite_update: At each iteration, rank candidates by cumulative predicted reward, select the top 8, and replace each horizon position's Gaussian mean and standard deviation with the elites' sample mean and standard deviation. No smoothing or minimum-standard-deviation extension is allowed.
planner.objective: cumulative deterministic ensemble-mean predicted environment reward
planner.action_selection: Use CEMPlanner's returned final Gaussian mean at horizon position zero and project it through the bounded action spec.

experience_and_learning.collection_policy: At each training-collection step, the agent assesses and executes a seeded uniform exploratory intended torque in [-2, 2]. Environment interaction must still produce the canonical decision, execution, observation, outcome, experience, and epistemic-transition records.
experience_and_learning.episode_reset_rule: Agent recurrent/belief episode state is reset at each environment reset. Real ExperienceEvent custody, replay lineage, persistent model state, optimizer state, and version ancestry remain continuous.
experience_and_learning.imagined_namespace_rule: Imagined MPC transitions are never ExperienceEvents and cannot enter real-experience or replay namespaces.

transaction.prepare: Clone live model and optimizer bytes; train the candidate using only the declared transition-ID multiset; do not mutate live state.
transaction.validate: Validate receipt ancestry, eligible split, target transformation, finite parameters, version predecessor, component digests, and held-out isolation.
transaction.commit: Atomically replace live model and optimizer state, advance the model/version ledger, attach the receipt and exact consumed-ID multiset, and derive a new compatible belief.
transaction.failure_invariant: Any rejected or interrupted pre-commit update leaves every live component byte-identical and emits no successful UpdateReceipt.

task_a_irrelevant_update.fork: exact cold model, optimizer, and model-version bytes
task_a_irrelevant_update.sampling_schedule: Reuse ensemble_bootstrap_a[0..4] and minibatch_order_a[0]. Because collect_a and collect_irrelevant both contain exactly 1,600 transitions, the decoded sampled-index tensor must be byte-identical to train_a.
task_a_irrelevant_update.isolation: The resulting irrelevant checkpoint is first required to improve held-out prediction on the oscillator itself, is then evaluated on held-out task A, and is never merged into the task-A/task-B agent.

rules: No episode or transition may occur in more than one split.
Only collect_a and collect_b are eligible for updates to the persistent A/B agent. collect_irrelevant is eligible only for the isolated train_a_irrelevant control.
Prediction-validation and behavior-evaluation episodes are held out from replay, optimizer state, normalization, calibration fitting, and hyperparameter selection.

Frozen and corrupted controls use task-A custody as declared. The oscillator arm instead uses its own causally independent real IDs with the same transition count and byte-identical optimizer index schedule, and must first pass a disjoint own-process predictive manipulation check. Every task-A evaluated checkpoint uses the exact paired validation and behavior reset seeds and planner budget.
A formal result must enumerate every episode ID and transition ID rather than reporting only counts.

collect_irrelevant.purpose: independent-oscillator training experience for the isolated nuisance-process control

predictive_validation_irrelevant.purpose: disjoint held-out manipulation check that the oscillator update learned its source process

seed_schedule.algorithm: seed(namespace, replicate_master_seed, index) = unsigned big-endian integer encoded by the first 4 bytes of SHA-256('WM-001|1.18.0|' + namespace + '|' + decimal(replicate_master_seed) + '|' + decimal(index))

master_seed_derivation.algorithm: master_seed(lane, index) = unsigned big-endian integer encoded by the first 4 bytes of SHA-256('WM-001|1.18.0|' + lane + '-master|' + decimal(index))

collision_audit.rule: Verifier and independent auditor must derive every current master and all namespace streams from the formulas, require the declared counts and zero internal master/master, stream/stream, and master/stream collisions, and compare all four master/stream cross classes against the exposed v1.0.0, v1.2.0, v1.3.0, v1.4.0, v1.5.0, v1.6.0, v1.7.0, v1.8.0, v1.9.0, v1.10.0, v1.11.0, v1.12.0, v1.13.0, v1.14.0, v1.15.0, v1.16.0, and v1.17.0 domains.

seed_schedule.pairing_rule: Within a replicate, all learned checkpoints and controls use the exact same task/split episode reset seeds. Planner randomness is reset to the same derived planner state before paired conditions.
seed_schedule.seed_record_rule: Every derived seed and RNG state digest must appear in the raw result. An undeclared RNG is a K0 failure.

budgets.matched_compute_rule: Task-A, corrupted-target, irrelevant-evidence, replay, and naive-sequential conditions use identical architecture, batch size, optimizer-step count, and planner budget. Task-A, corrupted-target, and irrelevant-evidence all fork the exact cold compound state. Task-A and irrelevant-evidence have equal 1,600-transition datasets and byte-identical optimizer sample-index schedules. Frozen performs zero optimizer steps but uses the identical evaluation planner budget.
budgets.budget_overrun_rule: Any formal overrun, early stop, extra data, extra optimizer step, or extra hyperparameter search fails K0. Resource telemetry may not redefine a budget after outcomes.

frozen.purpose: tests whether data collection or evaluation repetition alone explains improvement
frozen.implementation: clone the cold checkpoint; consume no gradient update; use identical held-out evaluation and MPC budgets

corrupted_target.purpose: tests whether correct transition linkage, rather than optimizer activity or target marginals, explains held-out predictive improvement
corrupted_target.implementation: apply one seeded permutation to the joint fixed-scaled (delta_cos_theta, delta_sin_theta, delta_angular_velocity, reward) training-target vector across collect_a while leaving inputs, learned-target marginals, transition counts, initialization, minibatch schedule, and update budget matched; its executed behavior is retained as descriptive evidence but is not a killing behavioral comparison

irrelevant_evidence.purpose: tests whether valid experience from this specified independent nuisance process plus matched optimizer activity is sufficient to explain the task-A gain
irrelevant_evidence.implementation: fork the exact cold compound state; collect 1,600 transitions from the independent phase oscillator in isolated custody; train 2,000 AdamW steps with task-A bootstrap and minibatch streams; preserve after-irrelevant bytes; require those bytes to improve NLL over cold on a separate 1,600-transition oscillator validation split; then evaluate the same bytes on the paired held-out task-A prediction and control budgets
irrelevant_evidence.manipulation_check: Across eight formal replicates, NLL_irrelevant(cold) - NLL_irrelevant(after_irrelevant) must have mean at least 0.05 nats/target-dimension and a two-sided 95% Student-t interval with lower bound strictly greater than zero.
irrelevant_evidence.hard_negative_note: The oscillator shares circular coordinates and is learnable by the same architecture, so generic representation transfer is possible. It simultaneously differs from task A in context, action causality, reward, velocity, and target marginals. Passing therefore supports only that task-A evidence beats this declared learned nuisance process; it is not a general theorem that every causally irrelevant dataset would lose.

naive_sequential.purpose: demonstrates actual A/B interference and supplies the no-retention baseline
naive_sequential.implementation: start from the exact post-A checkpoint and update for 2000 steps on collect_b only

random_policy.implementation: seeded uniform intended torque in [-2, 2] on the paired 32 evaluation resets

true_dynamics_mpc.purpose: planner and task ceiling under the same action/evaluation budget
true_dynamics_mpc.implementation: the identical CEM planner calls a separately namespaced exact semantic Pendulum transition and reward function; this oracle is never available to learned-model conditions or replay

execution_sequence.name: validate_irrelevant_learning_and_run_frozen_and_corrupted_a_controls
execution_sequence.required_comparison: held-out oscillator NLL of irrelevant checkpoint versus cold checkpoint

primary.name: heldout_mixture_nll_nats_per_target_dimension
primary.formula: -mean_over_transition_and_target(log(mean_over_5_members Normal(y; member_mean, member_variance))) on the four fixed-scaled targets

secondary.name: heldout_normalized_rmse
secondary.formula: root mean squared error of ensemble-mean predictions over fixed-scaled targets

secondary.name: heldout_90_percent_interval_coverage
secondary.formula: Decode the exact stored little-endian IEEE-754 binary32 target, member-mean, and member-log-variance tensors; promote each scalar exactly to CPython IEEE-754 binary64; in member-index order compute z=(target-mean)*math.exp(-0.5*log_variance), member_cdf=0.5*(1+math.erf(z/math.sqrt(2.0))), and mixture_pit=math.fsum(member_cdf_values)/5; classify the resulting finite binary64 value by its exact as_integer_ratio() representation as covered iff 20*numerator >= denominator and 20*numerator <= 19*denominator; report covered_target_count/coverage_target_count, with coverage_target_count=4*transition_count

primary.name: executed_undiscounted_episode_return
primary.formula: sum of the 200 real environment rewards

oracle_normalized_score.validity: oracle_mean_return must exceed random_mean_return by at least 100 return units

manipulation_check.name: irrelevant_source_nll_improvement_after_irrelevant_vs_cold
manipulation_check.formula: NLL_independent_oscillator(cold) - NLL_independent_oscillator(after_irrelevant) on the disjoint predictive_validation_irrelevant split

primary.name: a_return_improvement_after_a_vs_irrelevant
primary.formula: mean_return_A(after_a) - mean_return_A(after_irrelevant) on paired reset seeds

retention.name: seed_level_retained_a_gain_fraction
retention.formula: For each formal replicate i: (A_after_B_replay_i - A_cold_i) / (A_after_A_i - A_cold_i), using the 32-episode paired mean returns.
retention.validity: Every denominator must be positive; otherwise K6 fails.

inference.within_replicate: average the 32 paired episode-reset differences before across-replicate inference
inference.confidence_interval: two-sided 95% Student t interval across 8 replicate-level values using df=7 and t critical value 2.364624251
inference.multiple_testing: No multiplicity adjustment: every predeclared primary check is conjunctive and must pass; secondary metrics are descriptive unless named by a gate.
inference.missing_data: No imputation. A missing, non-finite, crashed, or excluded formal replicate fails its current killing gate.

k0_protocol_and_budget: formal binding was sealed before the first formal environment reset
exactly 8 complete formal replicates and every declared episode, transition, optimizer step, control, seed, and resource record are present

each replicate contains exactly 496 real episodes, 99,200 real transitions, 20 policy runs, 12 predictive rows, 7 evaluated checkpoints, 5 committed updates plus the rejected probe, and 5 optimizer manifests
source tree is clean at the bound commit and dependency/runtime hashes match the formal binding
k1_trace_and_isolation: 100% of persistent-agent learner-consumed transition IDs resolve to eligible collect_a or collect_b real ExperienceEvents; 100% of irrelevant-control IDs resolve only to collect_irrelevant
0 collect_irrelevant IDs, receipts, optimizer state, or checkpoint bytes occur in persistent A/B replay or the K7 checkpoint
0 prediction-validation or behavior-evaluation transition IDs occur in any replay sample, optimizer batch, fitted statistic, or candidate update
0 imagined transitions occur in the real-experience namespace
100% action-time model and parameter digests match the live committed checkpoint
k2_causal_update: post-A and post-B parameter digests differ from their predecessor digests
every successful UpdateReceipt binds the exact consumed transition-ID multiset, predecessor digest, candidate digest, and committed digest
a prespecified rejected-update probe leaves all live component bytes and versions identical
the first post-commit prediction and every later evaluation prediction bind the new committed parameter digest

k3_predictive_learning.after_a_coverage_decision_arithmetic: Across the eight task-A/after-A predictive rows, let C be the integer sum of independently verified interval_90_covered_target_count and T the integer sum of coverage_target_count (formally T=8*6400). The lower bound passes iff 10*C >= 7*T and the upper bound passes iff 100*C <= 99*T. Floating coverage fractions and their mean are descriptive only and never drive either gate.

required_exact_hash_matches: belief/runtime state at declared episode boundary
k7_restart.raw_trace_custody: Both the original live evaluation and fresh-process restored evaluation must be preserved as content-addressed canonical JSON sidecars. The independent auditor must reopen both, recompute all identity, prediction, action, return, process, reset-seed, and component comparisons, and reject reliance on producer-derived parity scalars.

killing_order.name: protocol, binding, completeness, and matched budgets
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. The run is ineligible for every WM-001 capability claim.

killing_order.name: trace integrity and held-out isolation
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Later numbers are contaminated or causally ungrounded.

killing_order.name: transactional causal state change and actual downstream use
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Version movement is not evidence that learned parameters changed safely or were used.

killing_order.name: held-out predictive learning
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Do not inspect behavior as evidence of world-model learning.

killing_order.name: executed held-out task-A improvement
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Prediction improvement did not produce useful behavior.

killing_order.name: task-B plasticity and demonstrated interference
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Retention is untested if B was not learned or the naive learner did not forget A.

killing_order.name: task-A retention without unacceptable task-B loss
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. Replay did not establish continual-learning retention.

killing_order.name: component-complete fresh-process persistence
killing_order.on_failure: Stop. The retained improvement was not shown to persist.

bindings.two_stage_seal: This scientific protocol and its outcome thresholds are sealed now. A separate formal-binding manifest may be created only after one qualifying full-budget development rehearsal, its independent audit attempt, and its canonical development-closure attempt are outer-finalized. The binding operator must consume that exact accepted closure attempt and publish exactly one canonical accepted binding attempt; a copied, directly created, failed, or unfinalized binding file cannot authorize formal execution. After binding acceptance, the standard-library launcher must consume exactly one binding-keyed accepted-binding rehearsal claim and publish an independently verifiable, same-inode outer-finalized accepted or failed terminal outside the formal namespace. Only an accepted terminal whose live claim and completion identities still verify may authorize formal dispatch; claim-only, failed, copied, mutated, or unfinalized evidence retires the version and cannot be retried. The binding must freeze the qualification, completed implementation, isolated execution mechanism, and complete runtime inventory before any formal outcome is generated. Binding implementation later may not alter this protocol.

required: protocol_sha256
protocol_version
result_schema_sha256
binding_schema_sha256
40-character Git commit SHA
clean worktree assertion
Git tree SHA
implementation manifest mapping every executed source file to SHA-256
captured independent-auditor, audit-runner, descriptor-bootstrap, experiment-runner, binding-verifier, and adjudicator source SHA-256 values
machine-readable adversarial test report SHA-256
environment-wrapper source SHA-256
Pendulum conformance-test report SHA-256
independent oscillator source SHA-256 and deterministic conformance-test report SHA-256
semantics identifier
exact CPython executable and platform identity
content-addressed conformance corpus and report
synthetic exact and adjacent 0.05 and 0.95 cases
the disclosed v1.3 boundary-coordinate float32 regression case
producer-reference and independent-auditor agreement
auditor-source and formal-test-report SHA-256
coverage_arithmetic.launch_rule: Formal launch requires every bound coverage conformance case to pass under the captured audit runner and exact runtime. The runtime and invocation manifests are always supplied through inherited already-open descriptors. The runner must produce byte-identical canonical reports when the captured auditor source is executed from its exclusive private path and from an inherited already-open descriptor.

dependencies.closure_rule: The formal binding must use a dedicated non-editable virtual environment with user-site disabled and no system-site inheritance. It must resolve the complete marker-selected transitive dependency closure of every non-Python root, reject duplicate canonical distribution names and editable installs, and require the installed canonical distribution-name set to equal that exact closure. Canonical names use PEP 503 normalization. Each distribution digest covers every stable file declared by RECORD, including unhashed declarations, while rejecting a missing declared file. The binding also records a complete regular-file and symlink inventory of each authorized package root so an unowned importable file or undeclared distribution cannot enter execution. Both producer and auditor run under -I -S -B, insert only the explicitly bound root, and therefore do not execute site.py, .pth files, sitecustomize, or usercustomize. Before any package-root or captured-auditor import, each launcher or bootstrap discards nonexistent absolute startup search entries, rejects every extant entry outside the inventoried standard-library tree, rejects site-package entries, and rewrites sys.path deterministically to the bound package roots followed only by canonical inventoried standard-library directories. Launch and adjudication recompute the exact closure and complete root inventory.
required: absolute CPython executable and executable SHA-256
complete stable standard-library root inventory SHA-256
deterministically sanitized module-search path with absent entries dropped and every extant ambient root rejected
explicit ordered package-root manifest
platform and accelerator identity
determinism flags
exact package versions
complete stable declared-file distribution SHA-256 values
complete package-root inventory SHA-256 values
complete transitive lockfile SHA-256
dependencies.version_policy: The protocol does not retrospectively choose package versions. The formal binding selects one exact compatible environment after implementation and freezes it before formal execution.

required: one captured descriptor-bound runner shared by prebinding conformance, launch-time conformance, official audit generation, and adjudication replay
CPython flags -I -S -B
sanitized environment with PYTHONPATH, PYTHONHOME, PYTHONUSERBASE, PYTHONSTARTUP, and PYTHONINSPECT removed and user-site disabled
manifest accepted from an inherited already-open descriptor
auditor source accepted from an exclusive already-open descriptor
captured child module-search path restricted to explicit package roots and canonical directories below the inventoried standard-library root

exact safe process environment fixed before dependency import, including CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=:4096:8, LAZY_LEGACY_OP=False, LC_ALL=C.UTF-8, PATH=/usr/bin:/bin, PYGAME_HIDE_SUPPORT_PROMPT=hide, SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dsp, and TZ=UTC
result-free sealed rehearsal imports Gymnasium, creates and closes Pendulum-v1 without reset or step, then rechecks exact process-environment and live bootstrap custody
producer_bootstrap.py captured as an explicit content-bound support file in every normal development audit and sealed formal outcome-audit runtime

result-free branch-exact restart-runtime validation proving the development captured-support branch and formal bound-source-snapshot branch select and verify their intended bootstrap bytes before any outcome audit; the independently bound expected bootstrap SHA-256, canonical report, and complete path-mode and descriptor-mode repeated-execution receipt must be content-addressed, retained, reopened, and byte-bound to the sealed preformal rehearsal and formal binding
path-mode and descriptor-mode canonical report byte equality
at least three repeated byte-identical prebinding executions
launch-time rerun immediately before launch-marker publication
no outcome artifact, result path, process identifier, wall-clock timestamp, or mutable absolute source path in the canonical conformance report
canonical audit-execution-failure record if the isolated process cannot emit its ordinary report
audit_execution.failure_rule: Any manifest mismatch, executable mismatch, ambient module-search entry, package-root or distribution mismatch, environment leak, source-descriptor mismatch, captured-stdout descriptor failure, noncanonical output, report-versus-return-code disagreement, or repeated-byte disagreement forbids binding or formal launch. The runner exposes no caller-selected or mutable output-path mode: every report is captured through an exclusive private regular-file stdout descriptor with a fixed OS-enforced byte ceiling, reopened under stable custody, and parsed before it can enter evidence. After a formal launch exists, the same failures retire the version and remain packageable only with a rejected disposition.

preformal_authorization.claim_rule: Generation exclusively creates the deterministic hidden sibling .preformal.staging and fsyncs its parent before the first command. Either that hidden claim or the final bundle consumes the one-shot attempt and forbids same-version retry.
preformal_authorization.publication_rule: Run all ten commands while the canonical bundle remains absent, stage exactly 20 content-addressed logs and one report under the hidden claim, then atomically rename that complete directory with no replacement. No other bundle member is permitted.
preformal_authorization.failure_rule: The report preserves every command result. all_pass exactly equals the conjunction of the ten zero exit statuses, ten exactly empty stderr streams, a clean post-command Git worktree, and stable pre/post Git, source, executable, package, runtime-seal, review, and input identities, plus strict semantic validation of accepted-closure and runtime-conformance stdout. Generation returns nonzero with passed=false, exact failed-command ordinals, names, and exit codes, and exact non-command failure identifiers for nonempty command stderr, post-run worktree drift, pre/post identity drift, accepted-closure semantics, or runtime-conformance semantics. Failed evidence can never authorize binding.

required: the sole fresh two-seed full-budget v1.18 development producer root
the development qualification records the protocol-bound deterministic matrix-contract SHA-256
complete passing producer verification with a same-inode outer-completion marker
one accepted canonical outer-finalized isolated independent engineering-audit attempt
byte-identical supplied and adjudication-mode audit reproduction
content-addressed development closure marker binding producer-manifest, result, audit, runner, runtime-manifest, Git commit, and Git tree digests
one accepted canonical outer-finalized development-closure attempt
development_qualification.performance_blinding_rule: The formal binding records development qualification identity and pass/fail engineering status only. It must not copy, compare, threshold, summarize, or select on K3-K6 development performance values.
development_qualification.closure_rule: The canonical v1.18 development producer path and attempt authorization are single-use. Creation of that producer root consumes the sole v1.18 development qualification, whether it later succeeds or fails; it may never be resumed, overwritten, or replaced by a sibling attempt. The development closure marker is exclusive and immutable. The producer, closure writer, same-process verifier, and fresh sealed-runtime verifier must all require the exact protocol-bound deterministic matrix-contract SHA-256. The closure operator must consume the accepted outer-finalized development audit attempt, stream and recheck every large producer member under typed descriptor custody, require strict JSON scalar types wherever booleans, integers, and floating-point values are semantically distinct, and publish only the exact byte-canonical USTAR qualification archive with canonical headers, member order, offsets, zero member padding, and canonical terminal records. It must publish the canonical closure attempt and register its terminal manifest with the outer launcher. A fresh sealed-runtime process must reopen and verify the accepted closure marker and attempt before any preformal report or formal binding is authorized. No development producer or audit may run after the closure marker is created for v1.18.0. Any failed qualification or subsequent source, dependency, runner, protocol, schema, or qualification change retires v1.18.0 and requires a new protocol version.

checkpoint.boundary: after a completed episode, with no pending action, environment side effect, candidate update, or partially consumed replay batch
required_component_states: replay index, priorities if any, sampling history, and eligible-ID sets
Python, NumPy, Torch CPU, Torch accelerator, collection, and planner RNG states
checkpoint.manifest_rule: The checkpoint manifest records media type, byte length, SHA-256, logical version, and ancestry for every component, plus an aggregate manifest SHA-256. Restore rejects a missing, extra, mismatched, or unknown stateful component.

development.purpose: claim-ineligible implementation, evidence, and custody rehearsal only
development.budgets: the complete pre-binding candidate rehearsal uses exactly the formal budgets; any smaller engineering diagnostic must be labeled and cannot satisfy the rehearsal gate
development.rule: Before binding, exactly one fresh complete two-seed candidate rehearsal at the canonical qualification-v1.18.0 path must satisfy only engineering, evidence, and custody criteria: schema and matrix completeness, deterministic execution, seed parity including exact central-verifier/independent-auditor equality for every retained prior-version master tuple, exact coverage counts and conformance, zero independent-audit failures or gaps, restart parity, isolated runner equivalence, reproducible adjudication-mode audit bytes, receipt-driven reproduction-sidecar joins, real producer-to-consumer composition and mutation coverage, package-verifier tests, production-scale streamed producer reconstruction with exact namespace and size bounds, terminal-bound archived result-qualification rejoin, canonical archive layout and strict type identity, bounded authenticated failure diagnostics, exact canonical formal-root enforcement, protocol-bound deterministic matrix-contract identity, cross-process hash-seed invariance, fresh sealed-runtime closure verification, launcher-owned accepted-binding rehearsal claim and terminal custody, formal-launch v3 authorization binding, and custody integrity. Exclusive creation of that producer root consumes the sole v1.18 development qualification before any outcome work; a refusal before producer-root creation is outcome-free and permits only correction of the invocation without changing protocol, implementation, environment, seal, dependency, seed, budget, or other bound bytes, while any failure after creation retires v1.18.0. The producer may never be resumed, overwritten, or replaced by a numbered or otherwise renamed sibling. Seed-override diagnostics, if used at all, are permitted only before the canonical producer exists, are permanently claim-ineligible, and cannot authorize qualification or tune a scientific value. K3-K6 development performance values are descriptive, permanently claim-ineligible, and neither a binding nor formal-launch criterion. Development may repair only the v1.18 evidence implementation named in the revision: protocol-authoritative independent rehearsal matrix validation, schema-exact production-projection fixtures, exact launcher-stored-byte independent acceptance, wrong-matrix rejection, versioned paths, fresh seeds, and preservation of all existing custody and authorization checks. It may not tune or change the model, training, optimizer, planner, controller, budget, controls, threshold, exclusion, metric, or gate. No development run may create a formal environment reset. After the exclusive development closure marker is created, no development run is permitted for this version. Any scientific change creates a new protocol version.

formal.budgets: exactly those declared in this protocol
formal.launch_rule: Formal execution is disabled until the canonical results/operator-v1.18/bindings/formal-binding-v1.18.0 attempt is accepted, its operator-attempt.json has a deterministic same-inode outer-completion marker, and its formal-binding.json exactly validates. After acceptance and before any formal root exists, the standard-library outer launcher must exclusively create results/operator-v1.18/rehearsals/accepted-binding-rehearsal-v1.18.0, write its fixed four sidecars, and hard-link rehearsal-claim.json as results/rehearsals/v1.18/accepted-binding-<formal-binding-sha256>.json before full authorization or child dispatch. The claim binds the exact accepted binding, launcher, producer bootstrap, canonical attempt, marker, and command preformal-runtime bootstrap-inventory-conformance --device <bound-device>. An existing claim, marker, terminal, or completion forbids a second child. Recovery after a consumed claim performs zero new dispatch: before canonical terminal publication it may outer-finalize only failed evidence, while an intact immutable canonical accepted or failed terminal may only be completed by hard-linking those exact bytes; no terminal may be rewritten or downgraded. A malformed or contradictory recovery state fails closed and remains terminal. A clean child must return zero with canonical binding-equal stdout, empty stderr, an empty inherited outer receipt, and every formal root, marker, producer, audit, review, adjudication, experience, update, and outcome path absent before and after dispatch. The launcher then publishes rehearsal-terminal.json as a deterministic same-inode outer completion. Intact accepted and failed terminal packages are authenticatable; claim-only, copied, mutated, malformed, and unfinalized states are detectable and rejected. Only the exact live accepted package authorizes formal execution; any other terminal state retires v1.18.0 and cannot be retried. The sole permitted formal output is exactly results/formal/<formal-binding-sha256>/confirmation-v1.18.0; omission, a timestamped or alternate child, a copied, directly created, failed, or unfinalized binding or rehearsal is rejected before producer-directory creation or formal-marker publication, and every verifier, auditor, and adjudicator independently requires that exact producer path. After accepted-rehearsal verification and immediately before the exact formal producer subprocess, the outer launcher must rescan every current-version formal namespace: the sole allowed entry is the canonical non-aliased empty results/formal/<formal-binding-sha256> parent prepared by the operator; any alternate confirmation-v1.18.0, marker, claim, audit or adjudication staging state, semantic review, symlink, non-directory, or nonempty prepared parent forbids dispatch. Before marker publication, the launcher must finish every fallible no-outcome operation: strictly reopen the accepted rehearsal package, preserve and verify the exact formal inputs plus copied binding-attempt terminal and completion bytes, verify the live binding and canonical attempt again, replay the bound prebinding conformance exactly once through the captured runner in descriptor mode, require its complete execution identity and canonical output bytes to match the bound repeated prebinding evidence, and precreate, fsync, and close the canonical producer-root formal-launch.json. The replay's isolated Pendulum resets are QA conformance operations: they collect no formal experience, train no model, write no result, and do not consume the formal attempt. That prospect.wm001.formal-launch.v3 record binds the canonical binding-attempt path, terminal-manifest digest, and outer-completion path and digest plus the accepted rehearsal's exact claim, claim-marker, terminal, and outer-completion path/byte/SHA-256 identities. Accepted rehearsal descriptors remain held and are rechecked immediately before and after the formal-launch record is hard-linked. Immediately before the first outcome-producing formal replicate/task reset, the launcher must atomically hard-link those exact launch bytes as the version-scoped protocol-wide results/formal/formal-launch-v1.18.0.json with no-replace semantics and fsync both directories. The two launch paths must be the same inode and have the same SHA-256. An existing v1.18.0 marker forbids launch under every v1.18.0 binding; markers for retired protocol versions remain immutable history and neither block nor authorize v1.18.0. The first outcome-producing v1.18 formal replicate/task reset begins the sole permitted formal attempt. It may never be resumed or rerun in place or in a new directory. On success or failure, the child finalizes producer-manifest.json; only after the child returns its logical status zero, one, or two, all captured inputs are rechecked, and the terminal receipt is verified does the outer launcher hard-link that exact manifest to its deterministic completion marker. The hard-link is the final logical commit; evidence lacking it is unfinalized and not citeable. A crash, missing evidence, producer-gate failure, audit-execution failure, independent-audit failure, package-verification failure, or semantic-review failure retires v1.18.0; no corrected audit may upgrade it and no same-version formal rerun is permitted. Once that reset occurs, no code, dependency, seed, budget, metric, threshold, exclusion, or analysis change is allowed for the binding.

required_granularity: one run record per replicate and condition
one episode record per executed real episode
one transition lineage record per training and predictive-validation transition

one predictive-metric record per condition carrying coverage_semantics='wm001-mixture-pit-binary64-count-v1', the exact integer interval_90_covered_target_count, coverage_target_count=4*transition_count, and interval_90_coverage=interval_90_covered_target_count/coverage_target_count
one optimizer-step ancestry record or content-addressed batch manifest per step
one checkpoint component record per stateful component
content-addressed canonical live and fresh-process restored evaluation traces
one gate observation per predeclared threshold
raw_evidence.content_hash_rule: Unless a binary tensor format is explicitly bound, hash UTF-8 JSON serialized with sorted keys, separators=(',', ':'), ensure_ascii=false, allow_nan=false, and no insignificant whitespace. A JSONL manifest hashes those canonical records in declared order, each followed by one LF byte.
raw_evidence.consumed_multiset_hash_rule: Each committed update records the unique phase-eligible canonical transition-ID set separately from consumed_sample_count. consumed_multiset_sha256 is SHA-256 over UTF-8 transition IDs in exact optimizer-consumption order, each followed by one LF byte; repetitions are retained. The phase-labeled content-addressed optimizer manifest must reproduce that order exactly.
raw_evidence.target_hash_rule: A transition target hash covers the fixed-scaled [delta_cos_theta, delta_sin_theta, delta_angular_velocity, reward] vector serialized as four little-endian IEEE-754 float64 values.
raw_evidence.prediction_target_binding_rule: The independent auditor must first require the result platform, device, and deterministic-algorithm setting to equal the pre-outcome runtime binding, require its live platform, machine, accelerator, thread settings, CUDA runtime and driver, cuBLAS setting, and installed dependency bytes to equal that binding, and select the formal arithmetic device only from that binding. It must then reconstruct every persisted prediction-sidecar target from the corresponding raw pre-observation, next-observation, and reward using the producer's exact operation sequence: binary64 delta=next-pre; binary64 reconstructed_next=pre+delta; round pre, reconstructed_next, and reward separately to binary32 on CPU; transfer those binary32 values to the binding's exact execution device; on that device perform binary32 subtraction and division by binary32 [2.0,2.0,16.0], and binary32 reward division by binary32 16.2736044. The resulting four little-endian binary32 values must be byte-identical to the sidecar target; no numeric tolerance, result-selected device, or cross-device substitution is permitted.
raw_evidence.aggregation_rule: The report must be mechanically derivable from raw evidence. A hand-entered aggregate without raw members is a K0 failure.
raw_evidence.producer_artifact_rule: Create a new exclusive producer-attempt directory before the first outcome-producing replicate/task reset. The accepted-binding rehearsal and formal prebinding replay may perform isolated QA-only conformance resets before marker publication; those resets collect no formal experience, train no model, write no result, and do not begin the formal attempt. For formal execution, the sole permitted producer directory is exactly results/formal/<formal-binding-sha256>/confirmation-v1.18.0. Every producer, verifier, auditor, and adjudicator independently requires that exact path and a live accepted outer-finalized rehearsal package for the same binding. Finish and verify every fallible no-outcome preparation first, precreate and fsync the canonical producer-root prospect.wm001.formal-launch.v3 record with the exact accepted rehearsal claim, claim-marker, terminal, and outer-completion identity rows, and publish it by same-inode hard link as the version-scoped protocol-wide formal-launch-v1.18.0.json immediately before any outcome-producing operation. Preserve raw JSON, protocol and schema copies, binding, development-qualification closure marker, the exact terminal-bound development-result-qualification.json sidecar and its formal-root copy, test and conformance reports, runtime manifest, captured runner/bootstrap/auditor/adjudicator bytes including producer_bootstrap.py as explicit normal/outcome-audit support, the exact bound implementation-source bytes, main and child process stdout/stderr, resource telemetry, checkpoint manifests, all content-addressed sidecars, and a complete producer manifest. The launcher and independent auditor must rejoin the streamed raw-result digest, archived qualification bytes, terminal-bound sidecar, copied sidecar, binding digest, and accepted rehearsal identity. Finalize this root before independent audit. Never resume, retry in place, repair, or overwrite it; failed attempts remain immutable evidence.
raw_evidence.restart_trace_rule: The producer must persist the original live evaluation and restored evaluation separately before computing a parity summary. The independent audit reopens both bounded canonical payloads and recomputes every comparison; producer summary zeros are never sufficient evidence.
raw_evidence.adjudication_package_rule: The independent audit must remain outside the outer-finalized producer root. Exactly one version-scoped formal-audit claim may be consumed. Its operator attempt must always reach accepted, rejected, or failure evidence and be outer-finalized; a report-generation failure consumes the claim and cannot be retried. Adjudication consumes only that exact canonical audit attempt. If it contains an ordinary report, adjudication executes exactly one bound replay and requires canonical byte equality; if it contains a canonical audit-execution failure, lacks outer completion, or otherwise has no ordinary report, adjudication executes zero replays and can publish only a rejected terminal package. Audit identity fields establish which evidence and implementation were examined; status fields separately record whether conformance, integrity, engineering completeness, and claim completeness passed. Therefore a coherent conformance-failing audit, or a canonical audit-execution-failure record when no ordinary report can be emitted, must remain publishable only as rejected evidence. After audit reproduction or canonical execution-failure capture and a separate version-2 semantic review, publish the one version-scoped adjudication claim and staged package by atomic no-replace operations. Its canonical manifest binds the finalized producer-manifest digest and completion identity, raw-result digest, formal-audit claim and attempt terminal identity, independent-audit or audit-execution-failure digest, runner/bootstrap/auditor/adjudicator source digests, runtime-manifest digest, semantic-review digest, formal-binding digest, binding-attempt identity, launch-record digest, assurance record, and explicit review disposition. A strict package verifier must reopen the final directory, reject missing, extra, symlinked, noncanonical, digest-mismatched, nonterminal, or disposition-inconsistent members, recompute the replay-count and disposition rules, and pass before any package is cited. The producer root, outer completion, formal audit attempt, semantic review, and verified adjudication package form the formal evidence; none may be overwritten in place.
raw_evidence.formal_artifact_rule: Formal evidence uses outer-finalized protocol-level append-only custody: one pre-outcome producer root, one version-scoped formal audit attempt, and one version-scoped external adjudication package. No producer output may claim support, and no accepted disposition is valid unless every terminal has its deterministic same-inode outer-completion marker, the external audit is complete and passing, and it independently recomputes K0 through K7.

auditor_must_recompute: all primary metrics from raw rows

every predictive coverage count independently from the persisted binary32 prediction tensors using wm001-mixture-pit-binary64-count-v1, with exact equality of the semantics identifier, covered count, total count, and derived fraction and with no target-count tolerance
fresh-process identity and parity by reopening both raw traces
oscillator-control environment dynamics, action independence, cold ancestry, optimizer-schedule matching, custody isolation, held-out own-process predictive learning, and task-A comparisons
independent_audit.claim_rule: README, roadmap, publication, or capability claims may cite WM-001 only after the pre-bound isolated runner's supplied audit report is reproduced byte-for-byte by a fresh adjudication-time run, that independent artifact audit and a separate adversarial semantic review accept all K0-K7 gates, and the atomic external accepted adjudication package passes strict post-publication verification while binding every evidence digest. A rejected package, audit-execution-failure record, automated zero-failure count, or self-reported producer pass is insufficient.
